---
title: Earth Microbiome Project
alias: &ALIAS earthmicrobiome
href: https://earthmicrobiome.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Earth Microbiome Project (EMP) is a massively collaborative effort to characterize global microbial diversity and function across Earth's environments using standardized protocols.

## Key Features

- Analyzed over 30,000 samples from diverse global environments using standardized 16S protocols
- Produced the Earth Microbiome Project Ontology (EMPO) for biome classification
- Provides open-access processed data including OTU/ASV tables, taxonomic summaries, and metadata
- Established reference sequences and primers (515F/806R) adopted as community standards
- Enables global-scale ecological comparisons of microbial community structure and diversity

## Use Cases in Research

- Characterizing environmental reservoirs of AMR genes across global habitats
- Benchmarking 16S amplicon analysis workflows against the EMP reference dataset
- Investigating microbial diversity gradients relevant to One Health AMR transmission
- Comparing gut, soil, and water microbiome communities within integrated AMR frameworks
- Using EMP metadata ontology for standardized environmental annotation in new studies

## Getting Started

Visit [Earth Microbiome Project](https://earthmicrobiome.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
